title: ELF: Embedded Language Flows
core_claim: ELF models language generation as continuous-time flow matching in embedding space, staying continuous until a final shared-weight discretization step, and claims better diffusion-language-model generation quality with fewer sampling steps than leading discrete and continuous DLM baselines.
likely_red_flags: Tech report and arXiv v1, not peer reviewed.
Main comparisons appear centered on diffusion-language-model baselines rather than full parity with strong autoregressive LMs.
Evaluation uses proxy generation metrics such as GPT-2-based perplexity and entropy; human preference or task utility evidence may be limited.
Compute and implementation are TPU/JAX-oriented, with PyTorch release still pending, which may slow independent replication.
Scaling evidence tops out at sub-billion-parameter models, so frontier-scale implications remain speculative.
